selected_security_runtime.session_transport: Secure HttpOnly SameSite=Lax cookie with opaque session ID
selected_security_runtime.secret_distribution: Managed secret store injected to backend and worker as environment variables
selected_security_runtime.local_development_secret_handling: Non-committed .env files for local development only

assumptions: The phase 1 technology baseline is React for the browser client, Go for backend API and worker services, PostgreSQL for relational storage, Redis for cache, and RabbitMQ for queue delivery.

The framework baseline is React + TypeScript + Vite with React Router, TanStack Query, and Zustand on the browser and chi-based Go services with pgx, sqlc, goose, go-redis, and amqp091-go on the backend.
The session baseline is a secure HttpOnly opaque cookie backed by Redis, and secrets are injected into backend and worker runtimes from a managed secret store.
All implementation targets referenced in this artifact are proposed and do not yet exist in the repository.
Phase 1 chat is authenticated-only and the hosted public model operates in non-explicit mode only.

milestones.title: Decision Lock And Runtime Baseline
exit_criteria: Selected framework and library mapping is encoded inside the React, Go, PostgreSQL, Redis, and RabbitMQ baseline.
Exact scaffold files and env-template targets are encoded in the planning baseline.
Lifecycle constants and the internal delete operating model are encoded.
Selected cookie-session and secret-distribution approach is encoded.

milestones.title: Foundation
exit_criteria: Repo structure and environment baseline exist.
Core persistence schema is in place.
MC seed data and discovery shell are ready.

milestones.title: Authenticated Chat Loop
exit_criteria: Delayed reply scheduling and worker processing are operational.
Provider access is isolated behind the adapter.

milestones.title: Continuity State
exit_criteria: Relationship state and restore behavior work for one Player-MC pair.

milestones.title: Policy, Lifecycle, And Security Hardening
exit_criteria: Hosted-model non-explicit policy and hard guardrails are enforced.
Lifecycle enforcement blocks inaccessible conversations and drives purge behavior.
Audit payloads are minimized and abuse controls are active.

milestones.title: Validation And Rollout
exit_criteria: Automated test layers cover required scenarios.
Release checklist exists for cost, latency, safety, and rollback review.

tasks.title: Encode selected framework and library baseline
acceptance_criteria: Repo conventions encode React + TypeScript + Vite, React Router, TanStack Query, Zustand, chi, pgx, sqlc, goose, go-redis, and amqp091-go.
The scaffold explicitly names package.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json, go.mod, sqlc.yaml, and cmd entrypoints for each deployable service.
Selected stack does not require architecture changes to authenticated chat, delayed replies, or provider abstraction.

tasks.title: Encode lifecycle constants and admin-delete operating model
acceptance_criteria: Retention durations are encoded as 12 months for conversation history, 30 days for derived-state purge after confirmed deletion, and 90 days for audit-minimum traces.
Internal or admin-managed delete flow is documented for phase 1.

tasks.title: Encode session and secret-distribution baseline
acceptance_criteria: Browser session transport is a secure HttpOnly opaque cookie backed by Redis.
Env templates define browser, backend, worker, and shared infrastructure keys for session, PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Facebook auth, and the LLM provider.
Secret-distribution approach uses managed-secret injection and remains consistent with server-only provider access.

tasks.title: Scaffold repo structure and environment baseline
acceptance_criteria: Top-level app, service, worker, test, and infra structure exists.
Exact scaffold files from the implementation blueprint exist at the selected paths.
Environment template placeholders are present for app, provider, PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, and lifecycle settings.

tasks.title: Implement core persistence models and migrations
acceptance_criteria: Schema covers identity, conversations, messages, memories, relationship state, reply schedules, and audit entities.
Player-MC ownership and score constraints are enforced.

tasks.title: Seed MC catalog
acceptance_criteria: Aira, Lyra, and Nova are available as seedable MC entries.
Seed data matches the PRD MC definitions.

tasks.title: Build browser shell, discovery routes, and login entry
acceptance_criteria: Responsive discovery flow supports mobile swipe and desktop list entry points.
Authenticated Players can enter the chat flow from discovery surfaces.

tasks.title: Implement session inspection and Facebook auth entrypoints
acceptance_criteria: Session inspection matches the API draft.
Facebook auth exchange resolves to internal Player identity.

tasks.title: Implement conversations and messages API slice
acceptance_criteria: Conversation resolve, load, and message send flows work with authenticated ownership checks.
Conversation access fails closed for missing, deleted, or purge-pending scopes.

tasks.title: Implement authenticated chat UI and refresh behavior
acceptance_criteria: Player messages can be sent from the chat screen.
Pending delay and delivered MC replies can be rendered.
Unauthenticated chat access is blocked with login-required UX.

tasks.title: Implement delayed reply scheduler and worker bootstrap
acceptance_criteria: Accepted Player messages create schedulable reply jobs.
Worker can process due jobs from the selected queue or cache layer.

tasks.title: Implement provider adapter and policy-mode hooks
acceptance_criteria: Provider requests and responses are normalized behind one adapter boundary.
The approved non-explicit phase 1 mode can be enforced without exposing provider specifics to other modules.

tasks.title: Implement short-term context refresh flow
acceptance_criteria: Recent context is condensed and available to reply generation.
Context remains conversation-scoped.

tasks.title: Implement long-term memory jobs
acceptance_criteria: Salient facts and milestones are written asynchronously.
Long-term memory never crosses Player-MC conversation boundaries.

tasks.title: Implement relationship state engine
acceptance_criteria: Deterministic score updates and threshold hints are persisted.
Scores remain within approved bounds.

tasks.title: Implement restore and continuity flow
acceptance_criteria: Returning authenticated Players can reopen the same MC conversation.
Memory and relationship continuity are restored for that conversation.

tasks.title: Implement hosted-model non-explicit policy and hard guardrails
acceptance_criteria: Hard guardrails are enforced.
Hosted-model output remains within the approved non-explicit phase 1 posture.

tasks.title: Implement lifecycle enforcement and admin-managed delete workflow
acceptance_criteria: Deleted or purge-pending conversations reject reads and writes in normal Player flows.
Delayed replies do not execute for deleted or purge-pending scopes.
Owner-bound transcript and derived data are purged within the approved window.

tasks.title: Implement audit minimization and privileged trace boundaries
acceptance_criteria: Audit payloads are metadata-first.
Audit storage does not duplicate full transcripts unless explicitly required.

tasks.title: Implement auth rate limiting and abuse-resistance controls
acceptance_criteria: Rate controls are enforced server-side.
Suspicious access attempts and repeated abuse patterns can be traced.

tasks.title: Implement automated test layers
acceptance_criteria: Required auth, delay, memory, relationship, policy, lifecycle, and delete scenarios are covered.
Test layers exist for module, integration, worker, and end-to-end behavior.

tasks.title: Run seeded environment smoke validation
acceptance_criteria: Seeded environment validates discovery, login, chat, delayed replies, memory continuity, and policy enforcement.
Observed failures are triaged before release readiness.

tasks.title: Prepare release checklist and observation baseline
acceptance_criteria: Release checklist exists for cost, latency, safety, and rollback review.
Observation baseline exists for provider errors, delayed replies, safety refusals, and lifecycle events.
